Question: A merry-go-round at a playground is rotating at 4.0 rev/min. Three children jump on and increase the moment of inertia of the merry-go-round/children rotating system by $$25 %$$ . What is the new rotation rate?
Answer: Using conservation of angular momentum, we have $$I ( 4.0 \text{rev/min} ) = 1.25 I \upomega_{\text{f}} , \upomega_{\text{f}} = \frac{1.0}{1.25} ( 4.0 \text{rev} \text{/} \text{min} ) = 3.2 \text{rev} \text{/} \text{min}$$
